rationale: Putin's public admission that Ukrainian attacks are damaging Russia's economy and society is a significant new signal for Ukrainians — it validates the effectiveness of their strategy but also raises concern about Russian desperation.

top_events.title: Putin admits Ukraine attacks hitting Russian economy and society
top_events.summary: Putin's rare public acknowledgment of domestic damage from Ukrainian strikes is a meaningful new development — it signals Russian vulnerability but also potential for escalatory responses affecting Ukrainian civilians.

top_events.title: Iran deal progress — US Congressman warns against money to Iran
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal could shift American diplomatic focus and resources, with indirect implications for the continuity of US support to Ukraine in the medium term.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: Escalating US-China friction adds background pressure on US foreign policy bandwidth, marginally relevant to Ukraine's interest in sustained Western attention and aid.
